children.name: Morus
children.description: Europe's largest seabird, plunge-diving from 30 metres at 100 km/h, closing its wings at the last moment. Air sacs beneath the skin and around the skull cushion the impact. Thousands pass along the Swedish west coast in autumn heading to Atlantic wintering areas.

children.name: Morus bassanus
children.description: Europe's largest seabird, plunge-diving from up to 30 metres with folded wings to spear fish far below the surface. Thousands stream past the Swedish west coast in autumn en route to Atlantic wintering grounds.

children.name: Morus capensis
children.description: A black-and-white gannet breeding on a handful of islands off South Africa and Namibia, distinguished by a long black throat stripe and golden crown.

children.name: Morus serrator
children.description: Breeds in dense colonies around New Zealand and southern Australia, famous for the accessible mainland gannetry at Cape Kidnappers.

children.name: Papasula abbotti
children.description: A rare, ancient booby now breeding only in the rainforest canopy of Christmas Island, nesting in tall trees rather than on the ground.

children.name: Sula dactylatra
children.description: The largest booby, white with a black mask and trailing wing edges, ranging across tropical oceans worldwide.

children.name: Sula granti
children.description: An eastern Pacific seabird of the Galápagos and offshore islands, formerly lumped with the Masked Booby and notorious for siblicide among chicks.

children.name: Sula nebouxii
children.description: Famed for its vivid turquoise-blue feet, which males flaunt in a high-stepping courtship dance; brighter feet signal a healthier mate.

children.name: Sula variegata
children.description: A key guano-producing seabird of the Humboldt Current, gathering in vast colonies along the Peruvian and Chilean coasts.

children.name: Sula leucogaster
children.description: A widespread pantropical booby, chocolate-brown above with a clean white belly, often seen perching on buoys and ships.

children.name: Sula sula
children.description: The smallest booby, with bright red feet and several colour morphs; uniquely among boobies it nests in trees and shrubs.
